demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care facility (nursing home); facility nursing staff present and assisting

contact_nok.relationship: facility nursing staff / next of kin on file

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: [Facility nurse calling] He threw up dark brown fluid all over his lunch tray, and his last bowel movement was pure black and sticky. He says he feels like he's going to pass out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Resident has vomited dark brown/coffee-ground looking fluid twice this morning and passed several black, tarry, very smelly stools over a few hours; he's now gone very pale and sweaty and nearly fainted when the staff tried to sit him up.

symptom_narrative.onset: started this morning, roughly 6 hours ago
symptom_narrative.character: vomiting dark brown fluid that looks like coffee grounds; bowel motions are black, tarry, sticky and extremely foul-smelling; feels very weak and lightheaded
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the nurse says he looks the worst she's seen him — pale, clammy and drained; he feels like he'll pass out
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: tummy feels a bit tender/sore across the upper belly; the faint, weak feeling is all over
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant weakness; the dizziness comes on hard whenever he tries to sit or stand up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying flat helps the dizziness a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: sitting up or trying to stand makes him feel like he'll faint
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: profound weakness, lightheadedness, cold hands, sweatiness, and a racing feeling; unable to get out of bed
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: none that the staff are aware of
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely bedbound today — too weak and dizzy to sit up or stand

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: severe arthritis (osteoarthritis), gets a lot of joint pain
past_medical_history.how_managed: takes strong daily anti-inflammatory tablets for the pain

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: 500 mg twice a day, every day

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: frail older man living in residential aged care; usually needs assistance; nursing staff manage his medications

objective_data_offered.timing: measured by facility staff during the call
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single supine reading on a facility automatic cuff; staff report he faints on sitting up so no standing reading was taken

objective_data_offered.timing: measured by facility staff during the call
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: facility monitor; fast and reported as feeling weak/thready by staff
facility fingertip oximeter; cold peripheries may affect the reading

objective_data_offered.value: resident looks very pale, sweaty and drowsy, lying completely flat in bed; hands look pale and feel cold to staff
dark brown, coffee-ground-like fluid over the lunch tray
black, tarry, sticky, very foul-smelling stool; incontinence pad heavily soiled
nearly fainted when staff tried to help him sit up
